usage_notes: Can be physical or digital
common_collocations: read a book, open a book, close a book
image_context: An open colorful book resting on a wooden table
example_1: I read a book every night.
example_1_translation: Eu leio um livro todas as noites.
example_3: Can I borrow your book tomorrow?
example_3_translation: Posso pegar emprestado o seu livro amanhã?
pronunciation_tips: The 'oo' sound is short, like in 'look'
memory_hook: A book has many 'hooks' of pages to read
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'look' which sounds similar but means different things

secondary_meaning: to give water to a plant
usage_notes: Uncountable noun, use 'a glass of water'
common_collocations: drink water, cold water, hot water
image_context: A tall glass of clear cold water with ice cubes
example_1_translation: Eu bebo água todos os dias.
example_3: Please give me a glass of water.
example_3_translation: Por favor, me dê um copo de água.
pronunciation_tips: First syllable sounds like 'war', second like 'ter'
memory_hook: Plants need water to grow 'war' tall
common_mistakes: Never say 'a water' - say 'a glass of water'

usage_notes: Refers to any residential building
common_collocations: my house, new house, small house
image_context: A small yellow house with a red door and garden
example_1_translation: A minha casa tem três cômodos.
example_2: We live in a big house.
example_2_translation: Nós moramos em uma casa grande.
example_3: The house is on the corner of the street.
example_3_translation: A casa fica na esquina da rua.
memory_hook: A mouse lives in a house, making 'house' sounds
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'house' (building) with 'home' (feeling/place)

primary_meaning: to put food in your mouth and chew
usage_notes: Past tense is 'ate', past participle 'eaten'
common_collocations: eat breakfast, eat food, eat well
image_context: A happy person eating a fresh green salad with a fork
example_1_translation: Eu tomo café da manhã às sete.
example_2_translation: Ela come arroz todos os dias.
example_3: We eat lunch together on Fridays.
example_3_translation: Nós almoçamos juntos nas sextas-feiras.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'eat' - the same as the word itself
memory_hook: When you eat, you say 'eeeh' it tastes good
common_mistakes: Remember past tense is 'ate' not 'eated'

primary_meaning: to move fast with your legs
usage_notes: Irregular verb - ran (past), run (past participle)
common_collocations: run fast, run home, run a business
image_context: A woman running on a path in a green park during sunrise
example_1: The child can run very fast.
example_1_translation: A criança consegue correr muito rápido.
example_2: I run every morning for exercise.
example_2_translation: Eu corro todas as manhãs para me exercitar.
example_3: Let's run to the store before it closes.
example_3_translation: Vamos correr para a loja antes que feche.
memory_hook: When you run, your heart goes 'run run run'
common_mistakes: Past tense is 'ran', not 'runned'

usage_notes: Use for physical size, compare with 'small'
common_collocations: big house, big problem, very big
image_context: A big gray elephant standing near a small tree
example_3: She has a big smile on her face.
example_3_translation: Ela tem um sorriso grande no rosto.
memory_hook: A big 'i' - big as the letter I
common_mistakes: Don't use 'big' for people - use 'tall' or 'large'

usage_notes: One of the first colors children learn
common_collocations: red car, red apple, red light
image_context: A bright red apple hanging from a green tree branch
example_3: Stop when the light is red.
example_3_translation: Pare quando a luz estiver vermelha.
memory_hook: Red means 'stop' - think of a red traffic light
common_mistakes: Remember the final 'd' sound - /red/

usage_notes: Can use 'mom' informally or 'mum' in British English
common_collocations: my mother, mother and father, mother dear
image_context: A mother hugging her happy child in a kitchen
example_2: I call my mother every day.
example_2_translation: Eu ligo para minha mãe todos os dias.
example_3: Mother is cooking dinner in the kitchen.
example_3_translation: A mãe está cozinhando o jantar na cozinha.
pronunciation_tips: The 'th' sounds like 'the' with your tongue between teeth
memory_hook: Mother rhymes with 'other' - your mother is my 'other'
common_mistakes: 'Mom' and 'mum' are informal - 'mother' is more formal

usage_notes: Used for counting and identifying single items
common_collocations: one apple, one time, one more
image_context: A single golden number one on a white background
example_2_translation: Me dê um livro, por favor.
example_3: There is only one cookie left.
example_3_translation: Só resta um biscoito.
memory_hook: You want 'won' thing - you want one thing
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'won' (past tense of win)

secondary_meaning: the time between sunrise and sunset
usage_notes: Compare with 'night' for the dark period
common_collocations: every day, new day, today
image_context: A beautiful sunny day with green trees and blue sky in a park
example_1: I go to school every day.
example_1_translation: Eu vou para a escola todos os dias.
example_3: Have a nice day!
example_3_translation: Tenha um bom dia!
memory_hook: A day has 'd' at the start and 'ay' sounds like 'yay'
common_mistakes: 'Everyday' is an adjective (everyday life), 'every day' is two words

usage_notes: Common color for sky, water, and objects
common_collocations: blue sky, blue eyes, blue car
image_search_query: bright blue sky with white clouds
image_context: A bright blue sky full of white fluffy clouds
example_3: I feel a bit blue today.
example_3_translation: Eu me sinto um pouco triste hoje.
memory_hook: The sky is 'blue' because it's 'blue'tiful
common_mistakes: Blue as 'sad' is informal - use 'sad' for formal contexts

primary_meaning: the part of your body at the end of your arm
usage_notes: We have two hands with five fingers each
common_collocations: right hand, left hand, hand in hand
image_context: An open human hand with palm facing up showing fingers
example_1: Raise your hand if you have a question.
example_1_translation: Levante a mão se você tiver uma pergunta.
example_3: Can you give me a hand with this box?
example_3_translation: Você pode me ajudar com esta caixa?
memory_hook: You 'hand' things to people with your hand
common_mistakes: Use 'hand' for humans, 'paw' for animals

primary_meaning: to move from one place to another
usage_notes: Irregular verb - went (past), gone (past participle)
common_collocations: go home, go to school, go now
image_context: A person walking forward on a path toward a destination
example_1: I go to school every day.
example_1_translation: Eu vou para a escola todos os dias.
example_3: What time does the bus go?
example_3_translation: A que horas o ônibus sai?
memory_hook: When you 'go', you say 'gooooo'
common_mistakes: Past tense is 'went', not 'goed'

primary_meaning: a person you know well and like
usage_notes: Friendship is important at A1 level
common_collocations: my friend, best friend, good friend
image_context: Two happy friends sitting together laughing over coffee
example_2: I have many friends at school.
example_2_translation: Eu tenho muitos amigos na escola.
example_3: A friend in need is a friend indeed.
example_3_translation: Amigo na necessidade é amigo de verdade.
memory_hook: A friend is someone who 'fricks' (frightens) your enemies away
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'family' - friends are people you choose

primary_meaning: a piece of furniture with a flat top
usage_notes: Used for eating, working, or placing things
common_collocations: dining table, table chair, on the table
image_search_query: wooden dining table set for dinner
image_context: A beautiful wooden dining table set for dinner with plates and glasses
example_2: Put the book on the table.
example_3: We have dinner at the table every night.
example_3_translation: Nós jantamos na mesa todas as noites.
memory_hook: A table has a 'table'cloth - 'ta-ta-ta'
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'table' (furniture) with 'desk' (for working)

primary_meaning: the color of grass and leaves
usage_notes: Natural color for plants, used in many expressions
common_collocations: green grass, green light, green eyes
image_context: Lush green grass field under a bright sunny sky
example_3: Turn right at the green light.
example_3_translation: Vire à direita no sinal verde.
memory_hook: Green like the grass you 'green' on
common_mistakes: 'Grean' is not a word - it must be 'green'

usage_notes: Can use 'dad' informally
common_collocations: my father, father and mother, dear father
image_context: A father playing football outdoors with his young son
example_2: I love my father very much.
example_3: Father is working in the garden today.
example_3_translation: O pai está trabalhando no jardim hoje.
memory_hook: Father 'fathers' the family - he 'fa-thers'
common_mistakes: 'Dad' is informal, 'father' is more formal

usage_notes: Used for counting objects or people
common_collocations: three times, three books, three friends
image_context: Three colorful balloons floating in the air red blue green
example_3: Three, two, one, go!
example_3_translation: Três, dois, um, vai!
pronunciation_tips: The 'th' is the same as in 'think', not 'the'
memory_hook: Three syllables in your mouth: 'th-re-e-e'
common_mistakes: 'Threw' sounds the same but means different - don't confuse

primary_meaning: to look at something with your eyes
usage_notes: Irregular verb - saw (past), seen (past participle)
common_collocations: see you later, see a movie, I see
image_context: A happy child looking up at a beautiful rainbow with amazement
example_1: I can see the ocean from here.
example_1_translation: Eu posso ver o oceano daqui.
example_3: She saw a beautiful bird.
example_3_translation: Ela viu um lindo pássaro.
memory_hook: When you 'see', you say 'seeeee'
common_mistakes: Past tense is 'saw', not 'seed'

usage_notes: One of the most common colors for objects
common_collocations: black cat, black car, black and white
image_context: A sleek black cat sitting on an old brick wall
example_3: The letter B is black.
example_3_translation: A letra B é preta.
memory_hook: Black is 'back' from all colors
common_mistakes: Use 'black' for colors, not 'dark' which is an adjective

usage_notes: One of the most common A1 emotions vocabulary
common_collocations: very happy, happy birthday, happy person
image_context: A happy smiling woman with bright eyes and warm expression
example_3: Happy birthday to you!
example_3_translation: Parabéns pra você!
memory_hook: You 'happed' upon something good - happy!
common_mistakes: Use 'I am happy' not 'I have happy' - emotions use 'be' verb

usage_notes: Used for describing objects and skin tone
common_collocations: white snow, white shirt, black and white
image_context: Beautiful white snow covering mountain peaks under blue sky
example_3: She is wearing a white dress today.
example_3_translation: Ela está usando um vestido branco hoje.
memory_hook: White is bright 'why-te'
common_mistakes: 'Whight' is not a word - only 'white'
